sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Check left panel buttons
w_objects = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'w']
print(f"Remaining gray (w) objects: {len(w_objects)}")

# Check bottom panels
print("\n=== Bottom panels ===")
for n in seg:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_row = min(p[0] for p in b)
    if min_row >= 50:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bounds=[{min_row},{max(p[0] for p in b)}]x[{min(p[1] for p in b)},{max(p[1] for p in b)}]")

# Check dark square in left panel
c_objects = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'c']
print(f"\nCharcoal objects: {len(c_objects)}")
for n in c_objects:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_row = min(p[0] for p in b)
    if min_row < 33:  # In the upper left panel area
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bounds=[{min_row},{max(p[0] for p in b)}]x[{min(p[1] for p in b)},{max(p[1] for p in b)}]")

# Check left panel button area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Left panel button area ===")
for i in range(33, 50):
    print(f"Row {i}: '{ascii_lines[i][6:25]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Before: Row 47-49 had "ggwggggwggggwggggwg" (gray "I" shapes)
- After: Row 47-49 has "ggBggggBggggBggggBg" (black "I" shapes)
So 4 buttons were cleared! The "w" count went from 20 to 16.
Also, the bottom panels swapped positions. The gray panel (B) is now on the left and the blue panel (b) is now on the right.
Wait, let me check the bottom panels more carefully:
- id=106: b (blue), bounds=[54,62]x[17,25] - this was at [7,15] before, now at [17,25]
- id=108: B (black), bounds=[55,61]x[8,14] - this was at [18,24] before, now at [8,14]
- id=109: B (black), bounds=[55,61]x[18,24] - this was at [8,14] before, now at [18,24]
So the bottom panels swapped! The blue panel moved from left to right, and the gray panel moved from right to left.
And the dark square in the left panel (id=13) is still there at [8,11]x[14,17].
So the mechanism is:
1. Click the yellow shape in the bottom panel
2. The bottom panels swap positions
3. Some left panel buttons get cleared (turned from "w" to "B")
Now I need to figure out which buttons get cleared. Looking at the change:
- Before: rows 47-49 had "w" (vertical bars)
- After: rows 47-49 have "B" (black vertical bars)
So the bottom row of vertical bars was cleared. This corresponds to the 4th row of buttons.
Wait, let me think about this more carefully. The left panel has:
- Row 33: horizontal bars "_" (always black "B")
- Rows 35-37: vertical bars "I" (gray "w")
- Row 39: horizontal bars "_" (gray "w")
- Rows 41-43: vertical bars "I" (gray "w")
- Row 45: horizontal bars "_" (gray "w")
- Rows 47-49: vertical bars "I" (now black "B")
So the pattern is: _, I, _, I, _, I
After clicking, the last "I" row (rows 47-49) turned black.
Now I need to click the yellow shape in the blue panel (which is now on the right) to clear more buttons.
Let me check where the yellow shape is now:
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - this is in the gray panel (now on the left at cols 8-14)
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me check the bounds again:
- id=108: B (black), bounds=[55,61]x[8,14] - gray panel, now on left
- id=109: B (black), bounds=[55,61]x[18,24] - another black panel
- id=106: b (blue), bounds=[54,62]x[17,25] - blue panel, now on right
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - yellow shape, in the blue panel (cols 20-22 is within 17-25)
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - yellow shape, in the gray panel (cols 8-9 is within 8-14)
So the yellow shapes are:
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - in the blue panel (right)
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - in the gray panel (left)
I clicked id=110 (yellow in gray panel, which was on the right before) and it cleared the bottom row of buttons.
Now I need to click id=111 (yellow in blue panel, which is now on the right)... wait, that doesn't match.
Actually, let me reconsider. Before the swap:
- Blue panel was on left (cols 7-15)
- Gray panel was on right (cols 18-24)
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - in gray panel (right)
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - in blue panel (left)
I clicked id=110 (yellow in gray panel, right), which caused:
1. Panels to swap (blue to right, gray to left)
2. Bottom row of left panel buttons to clear
Now after the swap:
- Gray panel is on left (cols 8-14)
- Blue panel is on right (cols 17-25)
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - now in blue panel (right)
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - now in gray panel (left)
So the yellow shapes moved with their panels. The yellow shape that was in the gray panel (id=110) is now in the blue panel (right), and the yellow shape that was in the blue panel (id=111) is now in the gray panel (left).
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me check the bounds again:
- id=108: B (black), bounds=[55,61]x[8,14] - this is a panel frame on the left
- id=109: B (black), bounds=[55,61]x[18,24] - this is a panel frame in the middle
- id=106: b (blue), bounds=[54,62]x[17,25] - this is the blue panel on the right
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - yellow shape at cols 20-22, which is within the blue panel (17-25)
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - yellow shape at cols 8-9, which is within the gray panel (8-14)
Hmm, but there are two black panel frames (id=108 and id=109). Let me check the ASCII to understand the layout better.
Actually, looking at the image, I can see:
- Left bottom: gray panel with yellow shape
- Right bottom: blue panel with yellow shape
- Far right bottom: blue blob
So the panels are at:
- Left: gray panel (cols ~8-14)
- Right: blue panel (cols ~17-25)
- Far right: blue blob (cols ~42-50)
The yellow shapes are:
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - in gray panel (left)
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - in blue panel (right)
Now, to clear more buttons, I should click the yellow shape in the blue panel (id=110, at [55,56]x[20,22], center ~ (55, 21)).
Wait, but I already clicked at (55, 21) before and that's what caused the swap. Let me check if clicking it again will clear more buttons.
Actually, looking at the pattern:
- Before: blue panel on left, gray panel on right
- After clicking yellow in gray panel (right): panels swapped, bottom row cleared
- Now: gray panel on left, blue panel on right
- Next: click yellow in blue panel (right) to swap again and clear another row?
Let me try clicking the yellow shape in the blue panel (id=110, at [55,56]x[20,22], center ~ (55, 21)).
But wait, the yellow shape id=110 is at [55,56]x[20,22], which is cols 20-22. The blue panel is at cols 17-25. So the yellow shape is in the blue panel.
Let me click at (55, 21) again.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking yellow in gray panel swapped bottom panels AND cleared the bottom row of left panel buttons (w count: 20→16). Panels now: gray on left, blue on right.
Goal model: Click yellow in blue panel (right) to clear more buttons.
Plan: Click yellow shape in blue panel at (55, 21).
